root_causes.name: Housing instability driven by speculative credit expansion
root_causes.intervention: Immediately tighten mortgage underwriting, require meaningful down payments and full documentation for owner-occupant and investor loans, and curb speculative lending in overheated metros through stricter supervisory guidance.
root_causes.predicted_outcome: Origination volumes and price appreciation should cool, inventory should stabilize rather than surge, and foreclosure pressure in the weakest housing markets should stop accelerating.

root_causes.name: Security boundary weakness in structured credit and wholesale funding
root_causes.intervention: Force banks and dealers to hold more common equity against mortgage-linked inventories, consolidate off-balance-sheet vehicles for capital purposes, and require conservative valuation and disclosure of structured-credit exposures.
root_causes.predicted_outcome: Dealer leverage should fall, issuance of opaque mortgage-linked securities should slow, and funding markets should demand smaller risk premia shocks when mortgage performance deteriorates.

root_causes.name: Economic fragility of households exposed to rate resets
root_causes.intervention: Mandate ability-to-repay underwriting based on fully reset payments, verify income and employment, and create an early refinancing or modification channel for borrowers facing imminent payment shock.
root_causes.predicted_outcome: Recent-vintage delinquency rates should level off, fewer borrowers should fail at reset dates, and consumer demand should prove less sensitive to housing and credit conditions.

root_causes.name: Originate-to-distribute incentives are degrading loan quality
root_causes.intervention: Align broker, lender, securitizer, and rating incentives by imposing repurchase liability for fraudulent loans, auditing stated-income pools, and tying compensation to medium-term loan performance rather than volume.
root_causes.predicted_outcome: Fraud incidence and low-documentation production should decline, securitized pools should contain fewer weak loans, and ratings should become more discriminating across mortgage collateral.
